ogTitle: Bossley Park vs Bostobrick: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bossley Park and Bostobrick.
heading: Bossley Park vs Bostobrick — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bossley Park, New South Wales and Bostobrick, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bossley Park has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Bostobrick (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bossley Park or Bostobrick safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bossley Park has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Bostobrick scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bossley Park scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Bostobrick scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bossley Park or Bostobrick?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bossley Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
